title: lippia lasiocalycina
intro: Lippia lasiocalycina, also known as Mexican oregano or Mexican lippia, is a plant species native to Mexico that belongs to the verbena family. The medicinal properties of Lippia lasiocalycina are attributed to its antiseptic, antifungal, and antibacterial properties, which are commonly used to treat various health issues. The plant has been used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats, as well as to treat digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions due to its health benefits. Lippia lasiocalycina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
